text_excerpt: Cat's Record and Local Action for Residents - Cat Smith - Cat Smith Skip to main content Cat Smith Working for Lancaster and Wyre Join Get Involved Donate About Data Protection Policy and Privacy Notice Expenses Contact Cat Contact Cat Register To Vote Local Team In Parliament Speeches and Questions Visit Parliament School Visits Local Priorities Proud and Accessible Communities Healthcare for Everyone Supporting Our Local and Rural Businesses Tackling the Climate and Ecological Emergency Get Support More Menu 23rd May, 2024 CatвАЩs Record and Local Action for Residents Serving as your Member of Parliament is a privilege. As we move into the General Election and I stand in the new Lancaster and Wyre area, IвАЩm reminded of some of the local achievements weвАЩve made over the past few years: рЯМН Secured a fracking-free Lancashire рЯП• Campaigned for a new local hospital рЯС©вАНвЪХпЄП Saved local cancer services рЯЪМ Extended rural bus routes рЯЪВ Won funding to restore axed rail line рЯПКвАНвЩВпЄП Reopened leisure facilities рЯТІ Held United Utilities to account on sewage рЯПГвАНвЩАпЄП Raised money for food banks and hospices рЯЪУ Enhanced local area police patrols рЯОЂ Protected rail ticket offices from closure рЯЫН Championed local small businesses рЯПЫ Preserved listed buildings and heritage рЯТ∞ Fought for environment disaster funds рЯУґ Fixed major mobile connectivity issues рЯТЄ Won compensation for affected customers рЯЪЃ Held park clean-up and community litter picks рЯС©вАНвЪЦпЄП Expanded local court capacity рЯСµ Improved Pension Credit take-up рЯЪЧ Stopped driving test centre closure рЯ©Ї Changed hospital policies to free up GPs рЯМК Successfully lobbied for flood defence funding рЯУЃ Challenged Royal Mail cuts & replaced boxes вЭМпЄП Stood against quarry and landfill plans рЯ•≥ Celebrated many community groups & events рЯЫ£ Had major roads resurfaced & potholes fixed рЯЪІ Improved road safety and speed enforcement рЯП° Called for park home/alternative energy help рЯСЈ Took dodgy developers to task рЯПШ Objected to housing without public services рЯТР Recognised local community champions рЯЩЛвАНвЩАпЄП Hosted Chats with Cat drop-in surgeries рЯСІ Spoke in schools on democracy & their futures рЯЪ™ Enjoyed countless conversations on doorsteps рЯС®вАНрЯС©вАНрЯСІвАНрЯС¶ Supported residents and local businesses This list is not mine, it bears the weight and power of thousands of your voices вАУ and itвАЩs an honour to represent them in Parliament. ItвАЩs a reminder of how lucky I am to have this opportunity. Thank you to all of you, especially those who will no longer be in the new constituency, such as the residents of Fleetwood and the Lower Lune Valley. As well as getting started on fixing our broken economy and public services, I hope to champion your priorities and aspirations for the next five years as your MP for Lancaster and Wyre. We live in a beautiful area. The hustle-and-bustle of an historic cultural city brimming with small independent businesses and a thriving higher education sector at the forefront of climate and health innovation, alongside the close-knit rural village life with renewable energy and sustainable agricultural vitality that keeps Britain powered, fed and connected вАУ and all this on the doorstep of the cracking views across national landscapes like Morecambe Bay, the Forest of Bowland and the Lake District. ItвАЩs a vision of what Britain can aspire to be when we come together and harness our potential. So letвАЩs roll up our sleeves and get the positive change our area deserves, and #VoteLabour! рЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМє вЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄПрЯМєвЭ§пЄП Cat Smith Contact details Lancaster Labour, Scotforth Road, Lancaster, UK View on Google Maps Email: cat.smith.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by James Groves on behalf of Cat Smith and Lancaster and Wyre Labour Party; all at 47 Scotforth Road, Lancaster, LA1 4SA ©2026 Copyright Cat Smith.
